Server._comment: TCP port the bridge listens on. Override with `--port N` on the command line; CLI wins over this value.

Tracing._comment: Per-request audit JSON capture. OFF by default — turn on to debug a specific issue, then turn off (the JSON files contain full request and response bodies, including user prompts). Each request emits 4 files (inbound-req, inbound-resp, upstream-req, upstream-resp) sharing the same seq. Directory is relative to the .exe unless absolute.

Routing._comment: nginx-style locations. Each location is a self-contained closure: 'When' matches (leaves: Model/Effort/Header; composites: AllOf/AnyOf; top-level fields = implicit AllOf), 'Use' fully describes what to do (Model swap, EffortMap remapping, Headers Set/Remove). First-match-wins; no chain, no fall-through. Header Set/Remove restricted to a whitelist (anthropic-beta, Editor-Version, Editor-Plugin-Version, Copilot-Integration-Id, X-GitHub-Api-Version). Use 'anthropic-beta:pattern' in Remove for token-level strip with trailing '*' wildcard. NOTE: the opus-4.6/4.7 '+1M beta -> dedicated -1m variant' redirects were REMOVED in the 2026 model reconciliation. Copilot retired the claude-opus-4.6-1m and claude-opus-4.7-1m-internal ids (they 400 'not available for integrator'), and the opus-4.6/4.7 BASE ids now serve 1M context natively (probed: 600k+ token prompts return 200), so no redirect is needed -- the 1M beta passes through and the base model handles it, same as opus-4.8 and sonnet-5.

Locations.Note: Codex alias: gpt-5.5-1m -> gpt-5.5. Codex applies a context-window cap to the literal id 'gpt-5.5'; naming it 'gpt-5.5-1m' on the Codex side (with model_context_window=1000000) sidesteps that client-side cap, and the bridge maps it back to the real model so Copilot's 1M-context gpt-5.5 is used. Normalize() keeps the '-1m' variant suffix, so the match must be on the full 'gpt-5.5-1m', not 'gpt-5.5'. gpt-5.5 natively accepts xhigh, so no EffortMap is needed.

OutboundBeta._comment: anthropic-beta tokens stripped from EVERY outbound request, regardless of model. Patterns use the same trailing-* wildcard as Headers.Remove. Operators on a different Copilot backend can edit this list — e.g. AWS Bedrock-only tenants who want structured_outputs back can delete the structured-outputs-* entry.

ResponseModelRewrite._comment: When true, rewrites `message.model` in the response (both buffered and the message_start SSE event) back to the model id the client originally requested — so Claude Code's session log and ccusage report claude-opus-4-8 instead of the back-end variant the bridge routed to (e.g. claude-opus-4.7-1m-internal). Turn off to let downstream tooling see the real back-end model.

UpstreamRetry._comment: Idempotent retry of transient upstream connection failures (net_http_client_execution_error / net_http_ssl_connection_failed) on POST /v1/messages. Only retries when the failure occurs BEFORE response headers arrive (request body never processed upstream, so re-sending is safe) — never mid-stream. MaxRetries=0 disables. Backoff: BaseDelayMs * BackoffMultiplier^(attempt-1), clamped to MaxDelayMs.

ToolLeakGuard._comment: Detects a Copilot-served Claude model leaking a tool call as literal <invoke name="X"><parameter…></parameter></invoke> XML inside a text/thinking block (not a real tool_use block), then forces the client to retry the turn cleanly. Detection is STRUCTURAL and requires ALL of: (1) a single block with a CLOSED, balanced <invoke>…</invoke> containing >=1 closed <parameter>; (2) the tool name is in the request's tools[]; (3) it is NOT inside a markdown ``` code fence. It does NOT key off the drifting prefix token ('court'/'call'), stop_reason (both tool_use and end_turn seen), or a bare unbalanced <invoke. Grounded in trace evidence: 5/229 (~2.2%) in a poisoned 3h session, all 5 closed and unfenced.
ToolLeakGuard._PreserveStream: true (default): keep streaming, inject the error mid-stream on detection (TTFT preserved; only dirty turns pay). false: buffer the whole response and emit a real HTTP status on a dirty turn — sacrifices streaming for ALL requests, not just dirty ones.
ToolLeakGuard._Signal: OverloadedError (default) => Anthropic overloaded_error; buffer-mode HTTP 529; Claude Code retries it and 3 consecutive trigger opus->Sonnet fallback. ApiError => generic api_error; buffer-mode HTTP 500; retried ~10x with no model fallback (mid-stream retryability less certain than overloaded_error).
ToolLeakGuard._ScanThinking: Also scan thinking blocks (default true). Trace leaks were all in text blocks, but thinking blocks can leak too.
ToolLeakGuard._MaxScanChars: Content-retention cap for BUFFERING detectors only (0 = unbounded). The tool-leak detector is a single-pass automaton that retains no content, so this does NOT affect it; it bounds a future JSON-repair-style detector that must hold a block's bytes. Exceeding it fails open (pass-through).
